description_long: This object is a robust, outdoor water cooler mounted on a thick, rectangular stone base with a slightly weathered appearance. The main column is cylindrical and made of a coarse, light brown stone, rising vertically from the center of the base. On two opposing sides of the column, there are small, square metallic panels, one of which appears to be a spout or dispensing area, likely for water access. The base is composed of two stacked stone slabs, with the upper slab showing some moss and water stains, indicating exposure to the elements. The overall design is utilitarian and vandal-resistant, suitable for public parks or sidewalks. There are no visible brand markings, cup holders, or water bottles attached, and the control interface is minimal, with only a metallic push panel or button visible.
description: A sturdy, stone water cooler with a cylindrical column on a thick rectangular base, featuring metallic dispensing panels and a weathered, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical stone column centered on a rectangular stone base, with no visible spout or panel.
description_view_1: This view reveals a metallic square panel or spout on the column, likely the main dispensing area.
description_view_2: Side view displays the column and base profile, with no visible spout or panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side to view 1, showing another metallic square panel, possibly a secondary spout or access point.
attribute_control_panel_design: Single metallic push panel integrated into the column, no visible buttons or displays.
